title: 64-Y Swift 3 Repulsorlift Sled

The '''64-Y Swift 3 Repulsorlift Sled''' was manufactured by [[Aratech Repulsor Company/Legends|Aratech Repulsor Company]].

==Characteristics==
Manufactured by [[Aratech Repulsor Company/Legends|Aratech Repulsor Company]], the 64-Y Swift 3 Repulsorlift Sled was a fast and light [[Hoversled/Legends|repulsorlift sled]] built with the same "engine with a seat" design philosophy as the [[Swoop/Legends|swoop]] bike.<ref name="ISB" /> Measuring 4.1 [[Meter/Legends|meters]],<ref name="ISB2" /> advanced engineering design principles removed the need for bulky [[Steering vane/Legends|directional vanes]] to assist in steering, allowing the single rider to sit in a more comfortable upright position, using handlebars and foot pedals.<ref name="ISB" /> Capable of reaching speeds of eight hundred [[Kilometer/Legends|kilometers]] an [[Standard hour/Legends|hour]] with a maximum flight ceiling of twenty-five meters,<ref name="ISB2" /> the Swift 3 was able to out-race anything short of an [[Airspeeder/Legends|airspeeder]]. The modular design of the Swift 3 allowed [[Engineer/Legends|engineers]] to refit the sled to deal with varying mission profiles.<ref name="ISB" /> The [[Repulsor coil/Legends|repulsor coils]] utilized by the Swift 3 were made of refined [[Durelium/Legends|durelium]], which was illegal for use in civilian vehicles.<ref name="SG">{{JournalCite|6|Swoop Gangs}}</ref>

The Swift 3 had a front-mounted [[Mk 2e/S medium blaster cannon]],<ref name="MBC" /> a single [[clamping hook launcher]], and [[drop net]]s that were launched from a compartment on the underside of the sled. The Swift 3's rider would position the vehicle over a target, then drop the nets. The nets tangled around the target vehicle, giving off short pulses to disrupt the target vehicle's repulsor fields. The net drew energy from the repulsor field, causing the target vehicle to crash. The sled was also equipped with up-to-date [[Sensor/Legends|scanning]] and communications [[Jamming beam/Legends|jamming]] equipment.<ref name="ISB" /> With a cargo capacity of four [[Kilogram/Legends|kilograms]]<ref name="ISB2" /> and a [[Standard day/Legends|day's]] worth of consumables, the Y-64 cost 8,000 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] to buy new, and 1,800 to purchase used.<ref name="TG" />

==History==
[[File:Swift_3.jpg|thumb|left|A stormtrooper riding a 64-Y Swift 3 Repulsorlift Sled]]
The Swift 3 was commissioned by [[Imperial Army/Legends|Army]] [[Army Command|Command]] to combat the rise in [[Swoop gang/Legends|swoop gangs]] terrorizing [[Planet/Legends|planets]] throughout the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]. The sled had an exceptional success rate, leading to the Swift 3 being deployed along [[Speeder bike/Legends|speeder bikes]] and [[All Terrain Scout Transport/Legends|All Terrain Scout Transport]] teams.<ref name="ISB" /> It was employed as a patrol craft on Imperial-controlled planets.<ref name="AE">{{SWMiniCite|set=Alliance and Empire|pack=Stormtrooper on Repulsor Sled}}</ref>

In the [[Standard month/Legends|months]] following the end of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], a 64-Y was in use on [[Sel Zonn Station]], an [[XQ2 Platform]] which orbited the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core World]] of [[Brentaal IV/Legends|Brentaal IV]]. It was employed by the Empire to move some special cargo and to spontaneously defend it from a group of [[Alderaanian Resistance]] agents.<ref name="TG" />

[[Brinaloy N'Vaari]], the [[Devaronian/Legends|Devaronian]] fiancée of [[A'jidre Skrigatov]], owned a heavily modified Swift 3 painted in flaming crimson adorned with black racing stripes. N'Vaari went into hiding to avoid marrying Skrigatov, paying [[Al T. Nadder]] to repaint the Swift 3 an iridescent green with purple racing stripes. She then traded the Swift 3 for a [[hydrobike]] at [[Phluche's Hydrobike Rentals]], paying the [[Herglic/Legends|Herglic]] owner [[Phluche Maltrid]] one hundred [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]] to not sell it for at least six [[Standard day/Legends|days]].<ref name="ARI">{{WEGCite|story=Abregado-Rae Intrigue|book=No Disintegrations (West End Games)}}</ref>

On the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Stend VI]], a specially formed [[Storm commando/Legends|Storm Commando]] unit, the [[Dark Riders]], was led by [[Lieutenant/Legends|Lieutenant]] [[Irlyn Resk]]. Tasked with combating the numerous swoop gangs on the planet, the unit utilized Swift 3s alongside modified [[Ikas-Adno/Legends|Ikas-Adno]] [[XR-10 Shadowhawk]]s.<ref name="Hives">''[[Wretched Hives of Scum & Villainy]]''</ref> [[Chop Harlison]], a swoop tech based in the city of [[Gallisport]] on [[Shesharile 5]], used the repulsor coils from a Swift 3 when building his custom prototype swoop, the [[Star Slinger]].<ref name="SG" />
